data.title: Bretton Fund exits UNP and RVTY
data.description:
• The Bretton Fund delivered a return of 1.44% in Q4, trailing the S&P 500’s 2.66% gain during the same period.
  • During Q4 2025, the fund exited Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP) and Revvity (NYSE:RVTY).
  • Source.

data.title: Armour outlines continued capital deployment and dividend stability while portfolio grows over 10% amid easing rates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ARMOUR Residential REIT, Inc. (ARR) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Scott Ulm highlighted a robust fourth quarter, stating "ARMOUR REIT delivered a robust fourth quarter, marking a 6.5% increase in book value in the fourth quarter. The strong growth extended to our balance sheet. The portfolio grew for a second consecutive quarter, increasing by more than 10% from the end of the third quarter of 2025, driven by roughly 22 basis points of spread tightening while maintaining moderate leverage throughout the quarter."
  • Ulm outlined that "ARMOUR's mortgage assets now total over $20 billion, supported by a strong capital liquidity position of approximately 54% of total shareholders' equity as of the end of January."
  • Ulm emphasized the company's continued high conviction in Agency MBS, describing the 2026 backdrop as "materially more supportive" due to declining rate volatility and easing funding costs, citing the Fed's ongoing easing cycle and a steeper yield curve.
  • CFO Gordon Harper reported "Q4 was a strong quarter for ARMOUR with a total economic return of 10.63% for the quarter as we benefited from MBS spreads tightening, lower MBS volatility and a lower interest rate environment. ARMOUR's Q4 GAAP net income available to common stockholders was $208.7 million or $1.86 per share. Net interest income was $50.4 million. Distributable earnings available to common stockholders was $79.8 million or $0.71 per common share."
  • Harper also noted "Quarter end book value was $18.63 per common share, up 6.5% from September 30. Our most recent current available estimate of book value as of Tuesday, February 17, was $18.37 per common share, which reflects the payment of our January dividend of $0.24 and the accrual of the entire February common dividend payable on February 27, 2026, again, of $0.24 per common share."
  • Ulm reiterated "We continue to set our dividend with a medium-term outlook. While acknowledging relatively tighter spreads versus the prior year, we expect the backdrop of a steeper yield curve and lower volatility remains supportive for a consistent and predictable return profile for our assets."
  • Co-Chief Investment Officer Desmond Macauley added that "ARMOUR's most recent net balance sheet duration stands at 0.14 years with a modest positive bias to the front end of the curve, consistent with easing monetary policy. Implied leverage, excluding treasury loans is 7.9 turns, a balanced posture that reflects tighter spreads and a lower volatility backdrop versus the prior year."

Outlook

  • Management stated that the market's appeal remains anchored in declining rate volatility and easing funding costs, supported by the Fed's efforts to lower rates and maintain ample banking liquidity.
  • Ulm indicated confidence in the dividend outlook, noting, "Our approach remains unchanged: stress test our liquidity, buy systematic hedging and deploy capital when opportunities present themselves. Overall, we're confident in our positioning, our strategy and our ability to deliver value for shareholders in 2026."
  • There was no explicit change in guidance language or new quantifiable forward projections compared to the previous quarter.

Financial Results

  • Harper reported "GAAP net income available to common stockholders was $208.7 million or $1.86 per share. Net interest income was $50.4 million. Distributable earnings available to common stockholders was $79.8 million or $0.71 per common share."
  • Book value per common share increased to $18.63 at quarter end, and the most recent estimate stood at $18.37 after accounting for declared dividends.
  • The company raised $3.8 million of capital through preferred stock and $138 million through common stock under an at-the-market program, issuing approximately 7.5 million shares of common stock and 230,000 shares of preferred stock.
  • ARMOUR paid $0.24 per common share per month in dividends for a total of $0.72 for the quarter.
  • The portfolio's aggregate prepayment rate averaged 11.1 CPR through Q4 2025 and Q1 2026 to date, compared to 8.1 CPR in Q3 2025.

Q&A

  • Timothy D'Agostino, B. Riley Securities: Asked about the outlook for portfolio and interest-bearing asset growth in 2026 after a year-over-year increase of about 49%. Ulm responded, "I got to tell you, it depends on how the market behaves, both on the investment side and the equity side, of whether we will be similar or smaller or in some other relationship to what we're able to do last year."
  • Trevor Cranston, Citizens JMP Securities: Asked about incremental returns on new investment and their comparison to the dividend. Macauley responded "on a carry basis, the levered yield on 30-year 5s, which are currently production coupon is around the mid-teens, let's say, about 15%. ... Now in terms of marginal capital raise, we see that, that hurdle rate is about 16%. ... you can see that for production coupon, the base case returns are close to that level already and with just a little bit more steeper and if we see more tightening, it would surpass that by a couple of more points."
  • Cranston also questioned the likelihood of further government actions to lower mortgage rates. Co-CIO Sergey Losyev said, "We do expect more announcements. ... But once you get to the implementation stage of it, things have been quite slow. Having said that, we definitely expect in the midterm here, for these announcements to be quite active."
  • David Storms, Stonegate Capital Partners: Inquired about liquidity and prepayment risk. Losyev stated "our liquidity, we mentioned is about 54% of the total equity at the month end. It's a really good spot, reflects our moderate leverage ... So the risk of faster prepayments has increased, right? ... So we feel like there's -- there are faster refinances are in the future, but we've built our portfolio to -- for that environment."
  • Eric Hagen, BTIG: Asked about MBS haircuts and coupon stack strategy. Losyev explained "we have worked with a lot of our counterparts to bring down the maximum haircuts closer to our weighted average of 2.75%. ... we've been looking away from current coupon because that's kind of where the biggest impact from the announcement has been really all throughout the Q4."
  • Hagen also asked about Fed cuts priced into the mortgage basis. Macauley replied, "The market is expecting by the end of December, a little bit over 2 cuts. And from our perspective, we think it's reasonable. ... If we do see more cuts, then funding costs will come down, the curve would steepen even more."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed a neutral to slightly optimistic tone, probing for growth sustainability, dividend outlook, and risk management, while pressing for clarity on capital deployment and prepayment risks.
  • Management's responses were confident and measured, with Ulm and Harper emphasizing stability and preparedness, and Macauley and Losyev providing detailed explanations of portfolio positioning and risk controls. Ulm stated, "Overall, we're confident in our positioning, our strategy and our ability to deliver value for shareholders in 2026."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analyst sentiment remained steady, with management slightly more confident and analysts continuing to focus on return sustainability and macro-driven risks.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management reported higher book value growth and net income versus the prior quarter, with book value per share up to $18.63 from $17.49, and GAAP net income available to common stockholders moving up from $156.3 million to $208.7 million.
  • The portfolio grew by more than 10% over the previous quarter, and aggregate prepayment rates rose from 8.1 CPR to 11.1 CPR.
  • Strategy remained focused on capital deployment during favorable conditions, with continued emphasis on Agency MBS, consistent dividend policy, and robust liquidity.
  • Analyst focus continued to center on incremental returns, dividend coverage, portfolio growth outlook, and risk management, with management reiterating confidence and providing detailed operational color.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted increased prepayment risk as mortgage rates drifted toward the lower end of the recent range, with Losyev stating "the risk of faster prepayments has increased, right? ... there are faster refinances are in the future, but we've built our portfolio to -- for that environment."
  • Analysts questioned the sustainability of incremental returns given spread tightening and the potential impact of future government policy moves on the mortgage market.
  • Funding market stability, haircuts, and the impact of further Fed cuts were discussed as ongoing areas of attention, with management emphasizing robust liquidity and diversified financing.

Final Takeaway

Management emphasized a strong fourth quarter driven by favorable market dynamics, portfolio growth, and robust capital liquidity, resulting in higher book value and net income. The leadership reiterated its commitment to maintaining a stable dividend and prudent capital deployment, highlighting confidence in the ability to deliver value for shareholders in 2026 despite evolving market risks and policy developments. The company remains focused on Agency MBS, disciplined risk management, and readiness to adapt to further changes in funding and prepayment environments.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Oakmark International Small-Cap Strategy adds VISIF; exits GRRMY, JDEPY, and MDMXF
data.description:
• Oakmark International Small-Cap Strategy (OAKEX) underperformed the benchmark in Q4 2025, returning 1.64% compared to the MSCI World ex USA Small-Cap Index’s 3.50%.
  • During Q4 2025, the fund initiated a new position in Visional (VISIF).
  • The fund exited Gerresheimer (GRRMY), JDE Peet's (JDEPY), and Medmix (MDMXF).
  • Source.

data.title: Kaiser Aluminum outlines 2026 EBITDA growth of 5%–15% with packaging and aerospace momentum
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Kaiser Aluminum Corporation (KALU) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Keith Harvey stated that "our fourth quarter results continue to build on the momentum we've established throughout the year. This marks our fifth consecutive quarter of performance ahead of our internal expectations and we exceeded the full year outlook we provided in October." Harvey highlighted that start-up costs moderated and metal pricing remained a tailwind. He underscored delivering more than 25% EBITDA growth for the year, with margins above 21% and record EBITDA in 2025, further noting, "we are positioned to harvest the returns from our recent investments, continue strengthening margins and generate free cash flow as we execute efficiently across the portfolio."
  • CFO Neal West reported, "our full year total net sales were $3.4 billion... our conversion revenue for the year was $1.5 billion, relatively consistent with 2024." West detailed, "adjusted EBITDA for the year was $310 million, up approximately $69 million from 2024," and noted improved margins to 21.3%. He added, "our net debt leverage ratio was 3.4x, an improvement from the 4.3x at December 31, 2024." West also explained, "2025 capital expenditures came in at $137 million," with major growth projects completed. He announced, "in 2025, we returned approximately $51 million to our shareholders through dividend payments."

Outlook

  • Harvey projected 2026 as a record year for both conversion revenue and EBITDA. For aerospace and high-strength products, he indicated shipments are expected to increase "in the range of 10% to 15% in 2026 with conversion revenue expected up approximately 5% to 10%." Packaging shipments are targeted to grow 5% to 10% and conversion revenue 15% to 20%. General engineering shipments and conversion revenue are expected to grow 3% to 5% year-over-year. Automotive shipments and conversion revenue are anticipated to decline by 5% to 10%, primarily due to planned outages, rather than demand. Harvey emphasized, "we are establishing an initial outlook for 2026 of 5% to 10% conversion revenue improvement year-over-year, with resulting EBITDA growth of 5% to 15%, setting the stage for another record EBITDA performance year for the company."

Financial Results

  • West reported operating income for 2025 was $189 million, with adjusted operating income at $188 million. Net income from 2025 was $113 million or $6.77 per diluted share, and adjusted net income was $100 million or $6.03 per diluted share. The company ended 2025 with $7 million in cash and $540 million net borrowing availability, for a liquidity position of $547 million. Full year capital expenditures were $137 million. Free cash flow for 2026 is anticipated in the range of $120 million to $140 million, dependent on metal price movement. The Board declared a quarterly dividend of $0.77 per share.

Q&A

  • William Peterson, JPMorgan, asked about the mix differences in the 2026 outlook for aerospace and packaging. Harvey explained, "we called out some specifics because, as you recall, we had an outage at Trentwood mainly through the third quarter... We expect to come back rapidly on the plate in this year... The prices have remained very strong and consistent."
  • Peterson inquired about the auto opportunity and capex guidance. Harvey replied, "these are actually fairly high-margin products for us. They're all specialty products... we're going to ramp up the investment... our automotive component here on very specialized products has the opportunity to increase substantially within the next 12 to 18 months."
  • Peterson queried about demand destruction and tariffs. Harvey responded, "We've seen absolutely no demand destruction in any of our product lines. We're seeing the general market, the general business start out very strong... All indications that we're getting are that what they're considering is more downstream type products and not removing the tariffs, but perhaps loosening tariffs but addressing the full end product versus just the raw material."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on growth outlooks, market mix, automotive opportunities, and cost structure, with a tone that was positive and inquisitive. Questions emphasized shipment recovery, capex increases, and market demand.
  • Management maintained a confident and upbeat tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, using phrases such as "I'm very encouraged by the momentum building" and "we are positioned for a very strong year." Harvey also addressed operational efficiency focus and dismissed concerns about demand destruction, reinforcing optimism.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the confidence level remained high, with management shifting from project execution to harvesting investment returns and operational leverage. Analysts' tone remained constructive, with less skepticism than in prior quarters.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for conversion revenue and EBITDA has shifted from flat to up 5% and 20%–25% EBITDA growth last quarter, to 5%–10% conversion revenue and 5%–15% EBITDA growth for 2026, with a focus on operational execution rather than metal pricing tailwinds.
  • Management’s tone became more forward-looking, emphasizing operational leverage and capacity expansion, especially in packaging and automotive specialty products. Analysts’ focus moved toward specifics of capacity ramp-up, capex allocation, and demand signals for 2026.
  • Key metrics such as EBITDA, margin improvement, and leverage ratio showed clear progression. The strategic focus evolved from project completion to cost efficiency and margin expansion.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted the risk of metal price volatility affecting working capital and free cash flow but expects operational gains to drive performance in 2026.
  • Harvey acknowledged that while metal pricing provided a tailwind in 2025, "our outlook did not assume that to continue throughout the year" in 2026.
  • Planned outages in automotive will impact shipments, but these are positioned as preparation for future growth rather than underlying demand issues.
  • No evidence of demand destruction was noted; management reported strong bookings and stable pricing across major product lines.

Final Takeaway

Kaiser Aluminum concluded 2025 with record EBITDA and strong margin improvement, citing robust demand in aerospace, packaging, and specialty automotive segments. Management projects continued gains into 2026, driven by recent capacity investments, a shift toward higher-value product mixes, and operational efficiencies, while remaining focused on cost control and cash flow generation. The company's proactive capital allocation and commitment to dividend payments signal confidence in sustaining growth and shareholder returns through the upcoming year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Crude inventory falls by 9M barrels for week ended February 13 – EIA
data.description:

data.title: Apple faces suit from W.Va. AG for allegedly failing to prevent child sex abuse images on iCloud
data.description:
West Virginia Attorney General JB McCuskey has filed a lawsuit against Apple (AAPL) for allegedly not stopping the storing of child sex abuse material, or CSAM, on its iCloud platform.

"Preserving the privacy of child predators is absolutely inexcusable. And more importantly, it violates West Virginia law," McCuskey said. "Since Apple has so far refused to police themselves and do the morally right thing, I am filing this lawsuit to demand Apple follow the law, report these images, and stop re-victimizing children by allowing these images to be stored and shared."

Apple shares were down 0.4% by noon trading on Thursday.

The suit was filed on Thursday morning in the Circuit Court of Mason County, West Virginia. It points out that under federal law, all U.S.-based technology companies are required to report detected CSAM to the National Center for Missing and Exploited Children (NCMEC).

It asserts Apple has failed to deploy adequate CSAM detection technology. For example, in 2023, Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) filed 1.47M reports to the NCMEC, while Meta Platforms (META) filed a staggering 30.6M reports. In contrast, Apple filed 267.

"As a direct and proximate result of its conduct, Apple is liable under multiple, independent theories of law, including strict liability for design defect, negligence for failing to implement adequate CSAM reporting technologies, creating or contributing to a public nuisance by facilitating the storage and hosting of unlawful CSAM content, and violations of the West Virginia Consumer Credit and Protection Act," reads the lawsuit.

The suit also points out that Apple announced a proprietary set of CSAM detection tools in 2021 that would automatically detect it and report it to the NCMEC. 

"However, by December 2022, in an effort to protect its brand and outsized smartphone and digital storage market share, Apple shelved the update and abandoned the project," the lawsuit said.

Apple contends it maintains the safest platform for children.

"At Apple, protecting the safety and privacy of our users, especially children, is central to what we do," Apple said in a statement to Seeking Alpha. "We are innovating every day to combat ever-evolving threats and maintain the safest, most trusted platform for kids. All of our industry-leading parental controls and features, like Communication Safety—which automatically intervenes on kids' devices when nudity is detected in Messages, shared Photos, AirDrop and even live FaceTime calls—are designed with the safety, security, and privacy of our users at their core."

Still, the lawsuit contends Apple's devotion to user privacy has led to a proliferation of unreported CSAM on its platforms.

"While Apple may believe its decisions further its narrative around privacy as a core company value, the reality is that Apple's decisions create market signals directed to users who value secrecy because they are engaged in high-risk activities," the suit said. "Apple knows that the intrinsic design of its products draws bad actors."

The suit calls on Apple "to take reasonable measures to abate the public nuisance its conduct has caused, created, and/or contributed to" as well as make "restitution, disgorgement, civil penalties, and all other pecuniary and monetary relief available under law."

data.title: Google unveils Gemini 3.1 Pro in latest update to flagship AI model
data.description:
• Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) unveiled the latest update to its flagship Gemini artificial intelligence on Thursday, showcasing Gemini 3.1 Pro.
  • “Building on the Gemini 3 series, 3.1 Pro represents a step forward in core reasoning,” Google said in a post on its website. “3.1 Pro is a smarter, more capable baseline for complex problem-solving. This is reflected in our progress on rigorous benchmarks. On ARC-AGI-2, a benchmark that evaluates a model’s ability to solve entirely new logic patterns, 3.1 Pro achieved a verified score of 77.1%. This is more than double the reasoning performance of 3 Pro.”
  • The new model is available starting today for developers via the Gemini API in 
  • Google AI Studio, Gemini CLI, the company's agentic development platform Google Antigravity, and Android Studio. It's also available for enterprises in Vertex AI and Gemini Enterprise and for consumers in the Gemini app and NotebookLM.
  • On its most recent earnings call, Google parent Alphabet said that Gemini had surpassed 740M monthly active users.
  • Shares of the tech giant were down fractionally in late morning trading.

data.title: Monument & Cathedral withdraws acquisition proposal for MarketWise shares
data.description:
Monument & Cathedral Holdings has withdrawn its unsolicited non-binding proposal to acquire the shares of MarketWise (MKTW).

In October 2025, the subscription services platform that provides financial research, software, education, and tools for investors had received a buyout proposal from Monument & Cathedral to acquire all remaining shares of the company for $17.25 per share in cash.

The withdrawal comes after feedback from the MarketWise board's special committee that the offer price per share undervalues the MKTW stock.

data.title: CRH outlines $8.1B–$8.5B 2026 EBITDA target amid record M&A activity and margin expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: CRH plc (CRH) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Jim Mintern highlighted a “record financial performance for 2025 with another year of double-digit growth in adjusted EBITDA and our 12th consecutive year of margin expansion,” noting the results were driven by disciplined capital allocation and the company’s embedded growth strategy. He stated, “We are pleased to announce a record financial performance for 2025 with another year of double-digit growth in adjusted EBITDA and our 12th consecutive year of margin expansion.”
  • Mintern detailed $4.1 billion invested in 38 acquisitions and $1.7 billion in growth CapEx projects, emphasizing, “Our ability to deploy capital in high-growth markets, integrate at scale and deliver unique synergies through our connected portfolio is a key differentiator for our business.”
  • The Board declared a quarterly dividend of $0.39 per share, a 5% increase over the prior year, and announced a new $300 million share buyback tranche.
  • CFO Nancy Buese reported a “robust balance sheet with a net debt to adjusted EBITDA ratio of 1.8x at year-end,” and stated, “We generated $5 billion of adjusted free cash flow in 2025, a strong performance representing a conversion ratio of 130% of net income and consistent with our 2030 financial targets.”
  • Buese also shared, “In 2025, we returned $1 billion in dividends, 6% ahead of the prior year on a per share basis and representing an increase of over 60% since 2019.”
  • COO Randy Lake described the integration of Eco Material as “progressing well with some good early wins on commercial, operational and logistical synergies.”

Outlook

  • Mintern projected full-year 2026 adjusted EBITDA between $8.1 billion and $8.5 billion, net income between $3.9 billion and $4.1 billion, and diluted EPS between $5.60 and $6.05. He emphasized, “Assuming normal seasonal weather patterns and no major dislocations in the political or macroeconomic environment, we expect full year adjusted EBITDA to be between $8.1 billion and $8.5 billion.”
  • Management expects continued strong demand in transportation, water infrastructure, and reindustrialization, with particular optimism for U.S. federal and state infrastructure funding and data center activity.
  • Randy Lake projected “ag volumes up low single digits, supported by mid-single-digit pricing” for the U.S., and similar low single-digit growth for cement both domestically and internationally. He added, “Our expectations for cement in the international business is volume up low single digits and pricing up low to mid-single digits.”
  • Buese reiterated, “We expect about $200 million of net incremental EBITDA in 2026 from those acquisitions.”

Financial Results

  • Total full year revenues reached $37.4 billion, up 5% over the prior year, with adjusted EBITDA at $7.7 billion, 11% ahead, and margins expanding by 100 basis points.
  • Diluted earnings per share grew 3% versus 2024, or 8% excluding one-off gains on divestitures.
  • Adjusted free cash flow was $5 billion, up 18% year-on-year.
  • Investments included $4.1 billion in acquisitions and $1.7 billion in growth CapEx, with $1.2 billion in share repurchases and $1 billion in dividends distributed.

Q&A

  • Adrian Huerta, JPMorgan: Asked about guidance assumptions and the impact of recent divestments. Mintern replied, “From a U.S. perspective, maybe first, really positive backdrop into 2026. And the key growth areas for us are really around transportation, water and reindustrialization.” Lake added, “Our future is really predicted a lot by our backlogs. It gives us kind of a 6- to 9-month view and bidding activity is strong.”
  • Michael Feniger, BofA: Inquired about prospects for a new highway bill and funding continuity. Mintern stated, “Federal highway funding for this year is at all-time record levels… the IIJA dollars are continuing to flow with over 50% of the highway funds yet to hit the street.” Lake described early legislative signals as “positive,” citing bipartisan support.
  • Shane Carberry, Goodbody: Sought detail on International Solutions’ margin gains. Mintern detailed, “Eastern European business… reflects our leading position in this region… strong underpin of infrastructural funding, mainly coming from the EU infrastructure fund, good reindustrialization activity.”
  • Angel Castillo, Morgan Stanley: Asked about Eco Material integration and cost inflation. Lake described integration as “off to a tremendous start,” with synergy realization “moving faster than anticipated.” Mintern noted ongoing inflation in labor and raw materials is driving the need for pricing momentum in 2026.
  • Keith Hughes, Truist: Queried Americas Building Solutions’ 2025 performance. Mintern credited “strong performance really came from the Infrastructure business,” highlighting optimization and performance initiatives.
  • David MacGregor, Longbow: Asked about M&A environment and synergy realization. Mintern emphasized, “the connected portfolio, which enables us to maybe identify synergies that maybe other parties cannot do in terms of transactions.”
  • Kathryn Thompson, Thompson Research: Questioned the impact of the connected portfolio on M&A pipeline and growth optionality. Mintern responded, “it’s the connected nature of the portfolio gives us the optionality where we deploy that capital across which markets, i.e., growth markets and then across the platforms.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were positive, focusing on guidance, M&A integration, and growth catalysts. Multiple questions pressed for details on margin drivers, M&A pipeline, and legislative developments, with no notably negative or skeptical tone.
  • Management’s prepared remarks were confident and highlighted strong results and a positive outlook. In Q&A, management remained positive, providing detailed explanations and emphasizing momentum, though references to “continued focus on pricing momentum” and “ongoing inflation” suggested awareness of challenges. Phrases such as “we are confident” and “good momentum” dominated the tone.
  • Compared to the prior quarter, sentiment was slightly more upbeat, with greater emphasis on visibility and robust funding backdrops, while both analysts and management maintained a constructive tone.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 was more specific, with adjusted EBITDA raised from $7.6–$7.7 billion in Q3 to $8.1–$8.5 billion in Q4. EPS guidance also increased.
  • Capital allocation activity accelerated, with acquisition spend rising to $4.1 billion (38 deals) from $3.5 billion (27 deals) the previous quarter.
  • Margin expansion and free cash flow both improved, with 12th consecutive year of margin increase confirmed.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted from general growth and margin expansion to specifics on M&A integration and legislative funding prospects.
  • Management’s tone remained confident, with increased emphasis on execution, synergy realization, and pipeline visibility for 2026.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited persistent inflation in labor, raw materials, and subcontracting as ongoing cost pressures, emphasizing the need for pricing momentum.
  • The residential new build market remains subdued due to affordability challenges, with management not expecting any “benefit from new build res in 2026.”
  • Political or macroeconomic disruptions and abnormal weather are flagged as potential risks to guidance.
  • Analysts sought clarity on funding continuity and legislative timing, reflecting external risks from potential delays in government infrastructure spending.

Final Takeaway

CRH’s call emphasized sustained momentum as the company enters 2026, supported by robust infrastructure demand, record M&A activity, and ongoing margin expansion. Management outlined clear financial targets, ongoing cost pressures, and a strong commitment to capital allocation discipline, positioning CRH to capitalize on megatrends in transportation, water, and reindustrialization while remaining vigilant to external risks and execution challenges.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Lexicon Pharma spikes after insider buy
data.description:
• Lexicon Pharmaceuticals (LXRX) traded higher on Thursday after the company’s chairman, Raymond Debbane, bought more than $350K worth of shares of the Woodlands, Texas-headquartered biopharma.
  • According to a regulatory filing on Wednesday, Debbane, who has served Lexicon (LXRX) as a director since 2007, has purchased roughly 268.5K shares of the company for an average price of $1.31 apiece.
  • The purchases valued at more than $352.9K were made via three transactions conducted over the past few days. Following the purchases. Debbane has increased his direct ownership in the company to more than 1.6M shares.
  • The transactions came after Lexicon (LXRX) shares climbed in late January after the company disclosed a successful meeting with the FDA regarding late-stage development of pilavapidin, its experimental therapy for diabetic peripheral neuropathic pain.
